title: One Joke to Rule them All? On the (Im)possibility of Generalizing Humor Detection
venue: Proceedings of the 2nd Workshop on Computational Humor (CHum 2026), pages 1–28
research_questions: RQ1: Do LLMs have the capability for humor transfer learning? Can they learn some type(s) of humor and generalize to new humor types?
RQ2: Between which types of humor is transfer most effective?
RQ3: How does data diversity influence humor transfer learning? Does training on more diverse datasets (e.g., containing multiple humor types) enhance generalization?

methods_proposed.name: Humor transfer learning evaluation framework
methods_proposed.summary: The paper proposes a framework for evaluating whether LLMs fine-tuned on one or more humor datasets can generalize to unseen humor types. It uses single-dataset, double-dataset, and triple-dataset training setups while keeping the training size fixed at 5,000 examples.

methods_proposed.name: Dad Jokes negative-example generation method
methods_proposed.summary: For Reddit Dad Jokes, which originally contains only humorous examples, the authors use GPT-4 Turbo in a few-shot setup to minimally modify lower-score jokes so that style and content are preserved while the humorous element is removed. They manually reviewed 3,000 outputs and report that only 2.63% failed to maintain style or content, with no examples retaining the punchline.

humor_theories.detail: The paper cites incongruity resolution as a shared mechanism that may provide common ground across joke types and discusses evidence from humans that resolvable and unresolvable humor involve different neural paths.
The related work notes that the Humor Styles Questionnaire defines humor styles with distinct social functions.

evaluation_methods.detail: Accuracy scores are the main metric for single-, double-, and triple-dataset training experiments; F1-score, recall, precision, standard deviations, and 95% confidence intervals are reported in appendices.
The authors manually reviewed 3,000 GPT-4 Turbo-generated Dad Jokes negative examples to assess generation quality.

headline_results.finding: In single-dataset training, Mistral achieved the highest reported transfer accuracy on an unseen dataset when trained on Amazon and tested on Headlines.
Training on Dad Jokes transferred strongly to One Liners, while the reverse direction was much weaker.
In double-dataset training, Amazon + Dad Jokes yielded the strongest transfer for Mistral on both held-out Headlines and One Liners.
headline_results.best_system: Mistral-7B trained on Amazon + Dad Jokes

headline_results.finding: In triple-dataset training, both models struggled most to transfer to Dad Jokes.
Increasing training diversity improved mean transfer accuracy from single to triple training for both models, with a larger average gain for LLaMA-2.
Reducing in-domain data to 33% in the triple-training setup caused only small average in-domain accuracy drops.

key_findings: LLMs can transfer some humor-detection ability across humor datasets, but transfer is asymmetric and depends strongly on the source and target humor type.
Dad Jokes is the strongest source for transfer across the studied datasets, but it is also the hardest target to generalize to from other humor types.
Amazon occupies a middle ground, transferring reasonably well and benefiting from Dad Jokes, while Headlines and One Liners are easier transfer targets but weaker sources.
Increasing training diversity generally improves transfer to unseen humor types, with diminishing returns after moving from single- to double-dataset training.
Mistral-7B consistently generalizes better than LLaMA-2-7B, although both models show the same broad transfer patterns.
In-domain performance remains relatively stable even when the amount of in-domain training data is reduced to 33% in the triple-dataset setup.
limitations: The study focuses exclusively on binary classification of short-form, English-language textual humor.
The study omits LLMs’ ability to rate the funniness of text, which would be valuable for dialogue or conversational humor.
The scope excludes multimodal formats such as memes and videos.
Each dataset is used as a stand-in for a humor style, but these assignments are approximate and do not capture the full nuance or variability of humor genres.
Individual datasets may reflect specific demographics, cultural biases, or artifactual noise.
The non-humorous Dad Jokes samples were generated by ChatGPT, so the transformation may reflect a more capable LLM’s implicit understanding of humor and may introduce systematic differences.
The analysis is based on only four datasets and two models, Mistral-7B and LLaMA-2-7B.
The methodology relies heavily on transfer learning and may not generalize to other approaches such as hybrid neural-symbolic systems.
safety_ethics_notes: Some datasets were collected from publicly available internet sources and may contain offensive content, but a brief examination found no indications of content exceeding the bounds of good taste. The datasets do not contain personally identifiable information except that Reddit Dad Jokes may include public Reddit usernames, which the authors did not use. All datasets were used according to their terms of use and solely for academic research purposes.

human_samples.observations: The paper says the Amazon dataset contains 19K records annotated for humor by humans; the study downsampled datasets to 6,250 examples before splitting.
human_samples.detail: Annotator count, annotator qualifications, recruitment, and annotation protocol are not reported in this paper.

human_samples.population: r/dadjokes Reddit users and Reddit score contributors
human_samples.observations: Positive Dad Jokes samples were selected as high-confidence positives using Reddit score ≥ 20; lower-score jokes were inputs for GPT-4 Turbo negative-example generation.
human_samples.detail: The paper does not report numbers of Reddit posters or voters, score distribution, or demographics.

human_samples.observations: 3,000 GPT-4 Turbo-generated Dad Jokes negative outputs were manually reviewed for style/content preservation and punchline removal.
human_samples.detail: The paper reports 2.63% failed to maintain style or content and no examples retained the punchline; number of reviewers, independence, blinding, and agreement statistics are not reported.

judges.name: Accuracy
judges.detail: Main transfer-learning results are accuracy scores on dataset labels, averaged over four training seeds.

judges.name: F1-score, recall, precision, standard deviation, and 95% confidence intervals
judges.detail: Reported in appendices using sklearn metrics with pos_label = 1; standard deviations and confidence intervals are computed across four seeds.

judges.name: Amazon Questions original human humor annotators
judges.detail: The paper only states that Amazon product-question records were annotated for humor by humans.

judges.name: Reddit score
judges.detail: Used to select high-confidence positive Dad Jokes samples with Reddit score ≥ 20.

judges.name: GPT-4 Turbo
judges.detail: Used in a few-shot setup to minimally modify lower-score Dad Jokes to create non-humorous negative examples while preserving style and content.

judges.name: Authors/manual reviewers
judges.detail: Manually reviewed 3,000 GPT-4 Turbo-generated Dad Jokes negatives for generation quality.

judges.name: One Liners source/collection labels
judges.detail: Humorous examples were collected using an algorithm designed to harvest funny one-liners; non-humorous examples came from news headlines, proverbs, and British National Corpus sentences.

judges.name: Sarcasm Headlines source labels
judges.detail: Dataset consists of real news headlines and sarcastic headlines from The Onion; this paper does not report a separate human annotation process for these labels.

generation_selection.scope: GPT-4 Turbo generation of Dad Jokes negative examples
generation_selection.detail: The paper describes generating negative examples by minimally modifying lower-score jokes, but does not report number of candidates per input, decoding settings, or whether multiple candidates were generated and selected. Manual review assessed quality rather than reporting a best-of-N selection procedure.

generation_selection.scope: LLaMA-2 and Mistral classification outputs during zero-shot and fine-tuned evaluation
generation_selection.n: Not explicitly reported; no multiple-candidate or best-of-N procedure is described.
generation_selection.detail: Evaluation used model.generate() with max_new_tokens = 5 to produce classification responses; results are averaged across training seeds, not selected among multiple generated candidates.

model_provenance.name: LLaMA-2-7B
model_provenance.detail: Loaded via AutoModelForCausalLM/AutoTokenizer, with 4-bit quantization; base version used for zero-shot validation and LoRA instruction-fine-tuned for main experiments. Exact revision/hash and inference dates are not reported.

model_provenance.name: Mistral-7B
model_provenance.detail: Loaded via AutoModelForCausalLM/AutoTokenizer, with 4-bit quantization; base version used for zero-shot validation and LoRA instruction-fine-tuned for main experiments. Also used as pretrained Mistral for embedding similarity analysis. Exact revision/hash and inference dates are not reported.

model_provenance.name: GPT-4 Turbo
model_provenance.detail: Used for few-shot generation of negative Dad Jokes examples; exact OpenAI model snapshot, API parameters, and generation dates are not reported.

model_provenance.name: ChatGPT
model_provenance.detail: Reported in Appendix M as writing/coding assistance only; not used as an empirical evaluator in the reported experiments.

model_provenance.name: GitHub Copilot
model_provenance.detail: Reported in Appendix M as writing/coding assistance only; not used as an empirical evaluator in the reported experiments.

model_provenance.name: Google Gemini
model_provenance.detail: Reported in Appendix M as writing/coding assistance only; not used as an empirical evaluator in the reported experiments.

inference_budgets.scope: Fine-tuning and hyperparameter search for LLaMA-2-7B and Mistral-7B
inference_budgets.sampling_parameters: Hyperparameter search over learning rate {3e-4, 5e-5, 6e-5}, LoRA rank {32, 64, 128}, and LoRA alpha {8, 16, 32}; 10 randomly sampled configurations per dataset from 27; 2 epochs; batch size 4 when possible and 2 for longer inputs; LoRA dropout = 0.1; gradient_accumulation_steps = 4; gradient_checkpointing = True.
inference_budgets.compute_or_cost: Training used a mix of A6000, A40, and L40S GPUs; training and evaluation of all experiments took approximately 14 days total. Monetary cost is not reported.
inference_budgets.detail: Single, double, and triple dataset experiments used 5,000 training examples. Main experiments used four random seeds: 7, 18, 28, and 42. Configurations were selected by median accuracy as described in Appendix E.

inference_budgets.scope: Generation-based classification evaluation for LLaMA-2 and Mistral
inference_budgets.detail: The paper reports use of model.generate() for evaluation but does not report decoding parameters such as temperature, top_p, do_sample, num_beams, or num_return_sequences.

inference_budgets.scope: GPT-4 Turbo Dad Jokes negative-example generation
inference_budgets.detail: Few-shot prompt is provided in Appendix A; exact call count, candidate count, context/token limits, decoding parameters, inference dates, and cost are not reported.

human_baselines.scope: Humor detection and cross-dataset transfer experiments
human_baselines.description: No human classifier or human transfer-learning baseline is reported. Human involvement is limited to pre-existing dataset labels and manual validation of generated Dad Jokes negatives.
human_baselines.detail: Model performance is compared across model/dataset training conditions and zero-shot model validation, not against humans under matched conditions.

mitigations: Zero-shot validation on the validation splits showed near-guess accuracy ranges of 40% to 56%, which the authors use to argue the models were not already strong on the tasks.
Random downsampling and train/validation/test splits were used before fine-tuning, so test examples were held out from the study's fine-tuning data.
Dad Jokes negative examples were generated for this study with GPT-4 Turbo and manually reviewed.
The authors performed domain classification to assess distinctness among datasets, though this addresses inter-dataset overlap rather than pretraining contamination.
contamination_risk.basis: The evaluated datasets are largely public legacy or web datasets: One Liners, Amazon Questions, Sarcasm Headlines, and Reddit Dad Jokes, with additional GPT-4 Turbo-generated negatives. Exact pretraining cutoffs, model revisions, and deduplication/decontamination against test examples are not reported. Some source data clearly predates the 2023 base models and other sources are contemporaneous or unclear. The paper addresses zero-shot familiarity but not benchmark contamination directly, so public-test contamination remains a moderate uncertainty.

llm_judge_dependence.affected_claims: No headline evaluation claim is based on an LLM judge scoring model outputs; however, Dad Jokes results depend on GPT-4 Turbo-generated negative examples as dataset construction.
llm_judge_dependence.human_validation: The authors manually reviewed 3,000 GPT-4 Turbo-generated negatives and reported 2.63% style/content failures and no retained punchlines.
llm_judge_dependence.detail: GPT-4 Turbo is used as a data generator for negative examples, not as a metric or evaluator of model outputs. Main results use automatic metrics against dataset labels.

reporting_gaps: Counts, demographics, geography, recruitment, and annotation protocols for original human annotators or Reddit voters are not reported in this paper.
Manual review protocol for the 3,000 generated Dad Jokes negatives is incomplete: number of reviewers, blinding, independence, adjudication, and inter-annotator agreement are not reported.
Exact GPT-4 Turbo model snapshot/version, API access method, inference dates, decoding parameters, candidate count per input, filtering policy after review, and cost are not reported.
Exact Hugging Face model revisions/hashes and inference dates for LLaMA-2-7B and Mistral-7B are not reported.
Evaluation decoding parameters beyond max_new_tokens = 5 are not reported, including temperature, top_p, do_sample, beams, and number of returned sequences.
The paper does not report a decontamination or deduplication check against the pretrained models' corpora or exact benchmark publicness relative to model training cutoffs.
The exact number of Dad Jokes inputs used for GPT-4 Turbo negative generation before downsampling/pairing is not reported.
No directly comparable human performance baseline is reported.
